title: Traffic Profile Schema
description: Schema for trafficgen traffic profile JSON files.  Supports two formats: a legacy single-profile format with a top-level 'streams' array, and a multi-profile format with named profiles each containing their own streams.

oneOf.description: Legacy single-profile format with a top-level streams array.
Multi-profile format with named profiles, each containing their own streams.

streamItem.description: A traffic stream definition specifying flow count, frame size, flow modifications, and other packet generation parameters.

flows.description: The number of unique network flows to generate for this stream.

frame_size.description: The Ethernet frame size in bytes for packets in this stream.

flow_mods.description: A function call string that defines how flows are differentiated by varying source/destination MAC, IP, port, or protocol fields.

rate.description: The transmission rate for this stream in millions of packets per second (Mpps).  A value of 0 means line rate.

frame_type.description: The type of Ethernet frame to generate.  'generic' is a standard data frame, 'icmp' generates ICMP packets, and 'garp' generates gratuitous ARP packets.

stream_types.description: An array of stream type classifications that control when and how this stream is used during testing.

items.description: A stream type classification.  'measurement' streams are used for throughput/latency measurement.  'teaching_warmup' and 'teaching_measurement' streams populate MAC/ARP tables.  'ddos' streams simulate denial-of-service traffic patterns.

latency.description: Whether to enable latency measurement for this stream.

latency_only.description: Whether this stream should only measure latency without contributing to the throughput measurement.

protocol.description: The transport protocol for packets in this stream.

traffic_direction.description: The direction of traffic flow.  'bidirectional' sends traffic in both directions simultaneously.  'unidirectional' sends from port 0 to port 1 only.  'revunidirectional' sends from port 1 to port 0 only.

stream_id.description: An optional identifier for this stream, used for tracking and referencing.

offset.description: The time offset in seconds from the start of the test before this stream begins transmitting.

duration.description: The duration in seconds for which this stream transmits.

repeat.description: Whether this stream should repeat after its duration expires.

repeat_delay.description: The delay in seconds between repetitions of this stream.

repeat_flows.description: Whether to reuse the same flow definitions on each repetition or generate new ones.

the_packet.description: A custom packet definition using Scapy syntax, prefixed with 'scapy:'.  Overrides the default packet construction.

device_pairs.description: An array of device pair specifications defining which TRex ports to use for this stream.  Each pair is formatted as 'tx_port:rx_port'.

enabled.description: Whether this stream is active.  Disabled streams are skipped during test execution.

legacyTrafficProfile.description: The legacy traffic profile format with a single top-level streams array.

streams.description: An array of traffic stream definitions.

multiProfileTrafficProfile.description: The multi-profile traffic profile format with named profiles, each containing their own streams.

default_profile.description: The name of the default profile to use when no specific profile is requested.

profiles.description: An array of named traffic profiles, each containing its own set of streams.

name.description: The profile name, used to select this profile at runtime.

streams.description: An array of traffic stream definitions for this profile.
